author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

`crates/jjf-storage/src/sync_v3.rs` classified `meta/*`
divergence as "not in a known v3 namespace" and returned an
`Error::Invalid` from the merge driver, exiting 1 even though
the issue/memory data had already landed. The `format-version`
sentinel is a presence flag (readers in
`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:1392` and
`crates/jjf/src/preflight.rs:83` check only that the ref
resolves; content is never inspected) so divergence is benign.

## Recommendation

Implemented as part of the F-001/002/003 bundle. Pull-side:
`meta/*` divergence routes to a no-op (keep local; the
classifier outcome doesn't bump the `merged` tally). Fetch
refspec gained a `+` prefix so non-FF remote-tracking updates
work too. The `format-version` ref still gets pushed by writers
(force-prefixed) so a fresh peer pulling from a new remote
still bootstraps a sentinel via scenario 1 (Copy).

## Confidence

HIGH. Verified in `pull_meta_sentinel_divergence_is_noop_not_error`
(diverged sentinel via deterministic `commit-tree`) and via the
manual repro at `scratchpad/qa/fix-verify/`: bob's `jjf pull
origin` exits 0 after his own `jjf init` planted a divergent
sentinel.

## Open follow-ups

None.
